name: Crock-o-Fennel-Cock-a-doo!
recipeInstructions:
1. If needed, defrost the chicken.
2. Remove any giblets for another dish.
3. Remove as much skin from the chicken as easily possible.
4. Place the chicken in the crockpot.
5. Wash the fennel and chop the leaves as needed.
6. Put the fennel bulb into the cavity of the chicken.
7. Scatter about 1/4 cup of chopped fennel leaves around the chicken.
8. Chop the leek (including leaves and bulb) and place it with the chicken.
9. Chop the carrots and scatter them with the chicken.
10. Pour water (or chicken broth) into the crockpot to cover the chicken.
11. Cook on high for 3 hours, then transfer the pot to the refrigerator to cool.
12. Once cooled, skim off all visible fat from the surface of the liquid.
13. Remove the chicken from the bones and place it back in the pot if desired.
14. Place the cooked meat back into the crockpot and cook on low for 4 hours.
This recipe is versatile; you can add any other vegetables you like, such as squash or pumpkin, or a can of cream-based soup for a different flavor profile.
Serves well with: baking soda biscuits (cut and pour the stew over them in a deep bowl) and a slice of fresh melon (Honeydew suggested).
Nutritional Information: When using water to prepare this dish, it is lower in fat than store-bought broths and stews (estimate 25% calories from fat or less), low-sodium, and sugar-free unless a cream-based soup version is used.
